title: Exodus: Plagues and Confrontation
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of exodus: plagues and confrontation and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study exodus: plagues and confrontation and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What was the tenth and final plague that broke Pharaoh's resistance and led him to release Israel? Answer: the death of every firstborn in Egypt.
segments.correct_answer: the death of the firstborn throughout Egypt
acceptable_variants: God killed the firstborn of Egypt
death of every firstborn son in egypt

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The repeated description in Exodus of Pharaoh's refusal to release Israel; both Pharaoh's own choice and God's judicial hardening are described, raising questions about divine sovereignty and human will
Ten divine judgments against Egypt targeting Egyptian gods and Pharaoh's authority, demonstrating Yahweh's supremacy over all rival claims to power
